title: GetResponse vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare GetResponse and Campaign Monitor. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool is right for your business.
h1: GetResponse vs Campaign Monitor: Detailed Comparison
intro: GetResponse and Campaign Monitor are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. GetResponse is a freemium all-in-one platform ideal for solopreneurs and course creators, while Campaign Monitor specializes in beautiful templates and multi-brand management for agencies. This guide breaks down their key differences to help you choose the right tool.
choose_a_if: You want a free tier to start without paying upfront
You need webinars, landing pages, or online courses integrated with email
You're a solopreneur or small business looking for an all-in-one solution
choose_b_if: You manage multiple brands and need dedicated multi-brand features
You prioritize email template design and visual appeal
You need transactional email capabilities for your business

faq.q: Is GetResponse better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: It depends on your needs. GetResponse is better for solopreneurs and creators due to its free tier, webinar features, and all-in-one platform. Campaign Monitor is better for agencies managing multiple brands with a focus on design. GetResponse scores slightly higher on reviews (4.2 vs 4.1 on G2).

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: GetResponse offers a free tier up to 500 subscribers, making it cheaper for beginners. Campaign Monitor starts at $11/month with no free option. However, Campaign Monitor's paid plans are competitively priced for agencies needing multi-brand management.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your contact lists and campaign data. GetResponse provides import tools to help migrate your subscribers and previous campaigns. Most migration can be completed within a few hours, though you may need to recreate some automation workflows manually.
